title: Corniola melia
intro:
There is no information available on the plant Corniola melia. However, I've found a plant called Corniola which is also known as Cornelian cherry or Cornelian cherry dogwood (Cornus mas).

Cornelian cherry (Cornus mas) is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe and Asia, known for its bright red fruits and medicinal properties. The health benefits of Cornelian cherry include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been linked to improved cardiovascular health, immune function, and protection against certain diseases. The bioactive constituents of Cornelian cherry include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and anthocyanins. The most common herbal preparations made with Cornelian cherry include teas, juices, and extracts, often used to support cardiovascular health and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Corniola melia is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the order Gentianales and the clade Asterids. Within the Rubiaceae family, Corniola melia is part of the tribe Psychotrieae. The plant is native to tropical regions and is characterized by its distinctive flowers and leaves. Its classification provides insight into its evolutionary relationships and genetic diversity.
uses: Corniola melia is known to have various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, such as fever, cough, and skin infections.
